title: Maharashtra weather update: Parbhani records 44 degrees Celsius as heatwave conditions grip state - Mid-day
full_text:
Maharashtra weather update: Parbhani records 44 degrees Celsius as heatwave conditions grip state
Aurangabad recorded 41.8 degrees Celsius, while Osmanabad touched 40.6 degrees Celsius. Jeur and Sholapur also remained above the 40-degree mark at 40.5 and 40.9 degrees Celsius, respectively. Udgir registered 40.8 degrees Celsius
The IMD data also showed warmer-than-normal night temperatures in several regions. File Pic
Maharashtra continued to reel under intense summer heat on Friday, with several districts in Vidarbha, Marathwada and north Maharashtra recording temperatures above 40 degrees Celsius, according to the India Meteorological Department’s (IMD) latest weather report issued at 8.30 am.
Parbhani hottest in the state
Parbhani emerged as the hottest location in the state with a maximum temperature of 44 degrees Celsius, followed by Jalgaon at 42 degrees Celsius and Malegaon at 41.2 degrees Celsius.
